title: This Florida Doctor’s Office Gave Its Own Patients Hepatitis C - Gizmodo
summary: The Florida Department of Health found that several patients of Lily J. Voepel caught the bloodborne infection while under her care.

title: New Study Reveals That Your Walks Should Actually Be This Long For Better Heart Health - AOL.com
summary: New research suggests that consistently walking for longer periods of time is better for your heart. The study authors and a cardiologist explain why.

title: Nearly Two-Thirds Of Young Americans Have Considered Leaving The Country - StudyFinds
summary: 63% of young Americans have considered leaving the U.S. AI anxiety also doubled among students, and half of adults report feeling lonely.

title: PHOTO ESSAY: One woman’s journey with lupus, the disease of 1,000 faces - AP News
summary: Ruth Wilson doesn’t look sick yet “everywhere hurts all the time” – because her immune system is attacking her own body.

title: Shyness linked to spontaneous activity in the brain’s cerebellum - PsyPost
summary: A new study reveals a connection between the personality trait of shyness and a brain region not typically associated with social behavior, offering new clues into its biological underpinnings.

title: When Thunderstorms Hit, Asthma Can Become an ER Nightmare - Gizmodo
summary: New research is the latest to find that thunderstorms can worsen your odds of a serious seasonal asthma attack.

title: Walking as few as 3,000 steps a day can slow Alzheimer’s progression, Mass General Brigham study says - The Boston Globe
summary: “Every step counts,” said lead author Wai-Ying Wendy Yau, MD, a Mass General Brigham neurologist.

title: How the psychiatrist who discovered SAD deals with seasonal depression - The Washington Post
summary: Norman Rosenthal uses light in his home to help with depression related to seasonal affective disorder (SAD). Spoiler alert: He has a lot of light boxes.

title: Bird flu: Expert tells farmers to prepare for the worst as virus spreads - BBC
summary: A top virology expert tells the BBC that the "super-infectious" avian influenza virus could mutate.

title: Scientists Found 2 Existing Drugs Can Reverse Alzheimer's Brain Damage in Mice - ScienceAlert
summary: In an effort to combat Alzheimer's disease, scientists are looking at existing drugs that could treat the condition, and a 2025 study identified two promising candidates that are currently used to treat cancer.

title: UK facing long, tough flu season, NHS chiefs warn - BBC
summary: Vulnerable urged to come forward for flu jab quickly as virus has come early this year.

title: Bird flu surges among poultry amid a scaled back federal response : Shots - Health News - NPR
summary: Migrating wild birds are spreading the virus to domesticated flocks, increasing the risk of eventually seeing a human outbreak. Scientists are troubled by the muted federal response.

title: Neural and molecular changes during a mind-body reconceptualization, meditation, and open label placebo healing intervention - Nature
summary: Editorial Summary: fMRI, multi-omics, and cellular assays reveal that an intensive mind-body retreat intervention modulates brain network connectivity and plasma signaling pathways linked to neuroplasticity, metabolism, and neurotransmission in healthy adults.

title: Should you treat a kid's fever? What doctors say as Trump Tylenol comments spark flu season debate - NBC News
summary: Doctors agree some fevers don't need to be treated, but insist there's no need to fear acetaminophen, the commonly used pain reliever found in Tylenol.

title: 'No other species can replace them': The drastic decline of the southern elephant sea - BBC
summary: The sudden decimation by bird flu of the world's largest seal has sent shockwaves through the scientific community, and the ecosystems shaped by these majestic animals.

title: AquaWomb: Artificial womb in glass tank could save premature babies - Interesting Engineering
summary: A Netherlands-based startup, Aqua Womb, is exploring the potential of making a womb-like life support system for extremely premature infants.

title: Scientists find hidden brain source that fuels dementia - ScienceDaily
summary: Weill Cornell researchers uncovered how free radicals from astrocyte mitochondria can fuel dementia. Using new compounds that target these radicals at their source, they slowed brain inflammation and neuronal damage in mice. The findings reveal a potential br…

title: New gel regrows tooth enamel and could transform dentistry - ScienceDaily
summary: Researchers have created a bioinspired gel that can regenerate tooth enamel by mimicking natural growth processes. The fluoride-free material forms a mineral-rich layer that restores enamel’s strength and structure while preventing decay. It can even repair e…

title: Everyday Chemical Linked to Liver Disease and Cancer, Study Warns - SciTechDaily
summary: A chemical commonly found in consumer products and used in dry cleaning has been shown to triple the risk of liver fibrosis. Liver disease is most often linked to three main causes: heavy alcohol use, fat buildup in the liver associated with obesity, diabetes…
